title: The End of the Snow Day
subtitle: 雪天变网课日：教育新趋势

paragraphs.en: Certain institutions, such as schools, are likely to close when bad weather, such as snow, flooding or extreme heat or cold, causes travel difficulties, power outages, or otherwise endangers public safety. When snowy weather arrives in the U.S., it means the chance of school children benefiting from the longstanding tradition of the snow day, when schools are forced to close and students get an unexpected day off.
paragraphs.zh: 当降雪、洪水或极端酷热严寒等恶劣天气导致出行困难、停电或以其他方式危及公共安全时，学校等机构可能会关闭。在美国，下雪天意味着学童有机会享受“雪假”这一悠久传统——学校被迫停课，学生意外获得一天假期。

paragraphs.en: The criterion for a snow day is primarily the inability of school buses to operate safely on their routes and danger to children who walk to school. Often, the school remains officially open even though buses do not run and classes are canceled. Severe weather that causes cancellation or delay is more likely in regions that are less able to handle the situation. Snow days are less common in more northern areas of the United States that are used to heavy winter snowfall, because municipalities are well equipped to clear roads and remove snow. In areas less accustomed to snow even small snowfalls of an inch or two may render roads unsafe.
paragraphs.zh: 雪假的标准主要是校车无法在路线上安全行驶，以及步行上学的儿童面临危险。通常，即使校车停运、课程取消，学校仍会官方保持开放。在应对能力较弱的地区，恶劣天气更易导致停课或延迟。美国北部习惯于冬季大雪的地区雪假较少，因为市政部门装备精良，能有效清理道路积雪。在不常下雪的地区，即便一两英寸的小雪也可能使道路变得不安全。

paragraphs.en: Snow days are a familiar theme in American film and TV shows, with children getting the good news and then running outside for some seasonal snowman-building and snowball throwing, against a background of joyful pop music. But the tradition is now over for pupils in several U.S. states such as South Carolina, Nevada, Georgia and Indiana. This academic year, many school boards have introduced policies which require students to work from home if the school is shut by snow or extreme weather. They are known as e-learning days, which certainly sounds less fun than a snow day.
paragraphs.zh: 雪假是美国影视作品中的常见主题：孩子们听到好消息后，在欢快流行音乐的背景下跑到户外堆雪人、打雪仗。但对南卡罗来纳、内华达、佐治亚和印第安纳等几个州的学生来说，这一传统已成过去。本学年，许多学校董事会出台政策，要求若学校因雪或极端天气关闭，学生须在家学习。这被称为“在线学习日”，听起来显然不如雪假有趣。

paragraphs.en: Teachers are also losing their snow days and instead will be expected to be on hand to take a virtual register and answer students' questions online. A pilot programme in a school district in Anderson County, South Carolina, has supplied students with electronic tablets loaded with assignments to complete in the event of a school closure. If it is successful, it could be rolled out across the state.
paragraphs.zh: 教师们也失去了雪假，取而代之的是需要在线进行虚拟点名并回答学生问题。南卡罗来纳州安德森县一个学区开展试点项目，为学生配备装载了作业的电子平板电脑，以备学校停课时完成。若试点成功，可能在全州推广。

paragraphs.en: But some parents object to the new policy if the vigorous debate on the Facebook page of Anderson County school district is anything to go by. 'When it snows, let the kids enjoy it,' said one commenter. Another said the decision would ruin school even more, and someone else called snow days a fun part of childhood. But supporters of the policy say it means children will miss fewer days of school. It will also bring to an end a less popular U.S. high school tradition: the make-up day, which requires students in many states to make up the time lost due to weather by working during school holidays.
paragraphs.zh: 但从安德森县学区Facebook页面上的激烈辩论来看，一些家长反对新政策。一位评论者说：“下雪时，让孩子们享受吧。”另一位称这一决定会“更毁学校”，还有人把雪假称为“童年的有趣部分”。但政策支持者表示，这意味着孩子缺课天数会减少。它还将终结一个不太受欢迎的美国高中传统——“补课日”，即要求许多州的学生在假期补回因天气损失的时间。

words.meaning: adj. 激烈的，充满活力的

paragraphs.en: Students in North Carolina already have several make-up days scheduled because of school closures during Hurricane Florence, which struck in September. Tom Wilson, the superintendent of Anderson County school district, said the change away from snow days makes practical and financial sense. He said technology has changed every profession, so it makes sense to use it to eliminate makeup days. Adam Baker of the Department of Education in Indiana said e-learning days were proving a great success. He said most Indiana schools already use digital devices during lessons, so it was an easy decision to extend this to days when schools are closed.
paragraphs.zh: 北卡罗来纳州的学生已因九月飓风“弗洛伦斯”期间的停课而安排了数个补课日。安德森县学区总监汤姆·威尔逊表示，取消雪假在实践和财务上都合理。他说技术已改变各行各业，用它来“消除”补课日是明智之举。印第安纳州教育部的亚当·贝克称，在线学习日正被证明“大获成功”。他说大多数印第安纳州学校已在课堂使用数字设备，因此将之延伸至停课日是个“简单的决定”。

paragraphs.en: He denies the decision is depriving children of the chance to enjoy the snow. 'Students are still able to enjoy snow days and outside time,' he said. 'Many have PE and science assignments that have them out enjoying the weather.' But local school superintendents in Ohio are resisting proposals to adopt e-learning days. They fear that students without internet access at home will be disadvantaged by the policy, and superintendent Tom Roth is concerned that e-learning days will offer a lower quality of education.
paragraphs.zh: 他否认这一决定剥夺了孩子享受雪天的机会，说：“学生们仍能享受雪天和户外时光。许多人有体育和科学作业要求他们到户外感受天气。”但俄亥俄州当地的学区总监们正抵制采纳在线学习日的提议。他们担心家中无网络的学生会因该政策处于不利地位，总监汤姆·罗斯则担忧在线学习日提供的教育质量会下降。

paragraphs.en: There are also so-called blizzard bags, with assignments that children take home ahead of an expected snow closure. But Mr Roth says it is not sufficient as a replacement. 'I think we still need the class time to give our kids the education that they deserve,' he said. 'You can't get that with a blizzard bag or doing the work from home like that. It's not going to be as effective.'
paragraphs.zh: 还有所谓的“暴风雪袋”，里面装着预计封雪前让孩子带回家的作业。但罗斯先生认为这不足以替代课堂。“我认为我们仍需课堂时间来给孩子们应得的教育，”他说，“靠暴风雪袋或那样在家学习是得不到的，效果不会一样好。”

paragraphs.en: There is a long-running debate on whether missing days of school affects attainment. In England, there has been a focus on tackling absenteeism from school. The Department for Education (DFE) published research in 2016 arguing that missing any days at school could have a negative impact on results. Even a few days lost in a year could be enough to miss out on getting a good exam grade, the DFE's research concluded. This differed from the findings of a study from Harvard University in the U.S., which concluded that missing a few occasional days because of the weather did not damage learning.
paragraphs.zh: 关于缺课是否影响学业成绩的辩论由来已久。在英国，解决旷课问题一直是重点。教育部2016年发布的研究称，缺课任何一天都可能对成绩产生负面影响。研究结论指出，一年中即便只缺几天课，也足以错失好成绩。这与美国哈佛大学一项研究的发现相左，后者认为因天气偶尔缺课几天并不损害学习。

paragraphs.en: The Harvard study examined seven years of school results data and could not find any impact from snow closures. What caused more disruption was when schools tried to stay open in bad weather, even though many staff and pupils were absent. But weather can make a difference to school results, according to another piece of Harvard research published last summer. It's hot weather that has the negative impact. The results of 10 million school students were examined over 13 years and researchers found a significant link between years with extremely hot weather and lower results.
paragraphs.zh: 哈佛大学的研究考察了七年学校成绩数据，未发现雪天停课有任何影响。造成更大干扰的是学校在恶劣天气下仍试图开放，尽管许多师生缺席。但据哈佛去年夏天发表的另一项研究，天气确实会影响学校成绩——产生负面影响的是炎热天气。研究人员审视了13年间1000万名学生的成绩，发现极端炎热年份与较低成绩之间存在显著关联。

paragraphs.en: It's obvious that students should go to school every day to get the most out of education. In cases of extreme weather students don't always have that option. However, research shows that authorised absences from school such as during extreme weather are less problematic for students than absences that are not authorised. This is because unauthorised absences tend to reflect patterns and behaviours of student disengagement, or the possible negative attitudes of parents towards education that students adopt and carry with them through schooling. The level of impact on students' educational performance is all to do with the length of time that a student is absent from school and how regularly this occurs.
paragraphs.zh: 显然，学生应每天上学以充分受益于教育。极端天气下学生并非总有选择。然而研究表明，因极端天气等获准的缺勤对学生的影响小于未经批准的缺勤。这是因为未经批准的缺勤往往反映出学生脱离学校的模式与行为，或家长对教育的消极态度被学生内化并带入求学过程。对学生学业表现的影响程度完全取决于缺课时长及其发生频率。
